nativeRange.habitat: Shallow reef flats and intertidal zones in Hawaiian coastal waters, at depths from the surface to around 15 meters.

avoidWith: heavily grazing herbivores that may consume the entire specimen before it can grow out

imageCandidates.notes: Photo by Coughdrop12, 4032x3024. Sharp in-tank shot under blue reef lighting showing the whole bushy, densely branched burgundy-red thallus; best display representation.
Photo by Coughdrop12, 4032x3024. Bushy red-to-burgundy branched clump growing on rock; some green macroalgae nearby but target species central and in colour.
Photo by USFWS Fish and Aquatic Conservation, 1024x768. Hand-held sprig clearly showing the diagnostic branching thallus structure; browner/out-of-water so kept as a non-recommended ID extra.

summary: Red Ogo is a fast-growing Hawaiian red macroalgae valued for its ability to absorb excess nutrients from the water and its use as a live food supplement for tangs and other herbivorous fish. It is generally considered one of the easier macroalgae species to cultivate.
careNotes:
Red Ogo (Gracilaria parvispora) is a rhodophyte (red alga) native to Hawaiian coastal waters, where it grows on shallow reef flats. It is widely kept in the marine hobby for its fast growth, tolerance of typical reef parameters, and dual-use value as both a nutrient-exporting macroalgae and a live food source.

Water temperature is generally advised in the range of 22–26°C (72–78°F). Salinity of 1.023–1.025 SG is broadly accepted for this species. Alkalinity is typically kept at 8–12 dKH, consistent with standard reef parameters.

Red Ogo grows as a short, bushy, densely branched thallus with bright red to burgundy coloration. Under well-maintained reef conditions it tends to grow rapidly — one keeper documented harvesting approximately two softball-sized clumps within a month while maintaining a healthy base culture. Periodic harvesting is advised to prevent overgrowth and to export the nutrients the algae has absorbed.

Red Ogo is commonly grown in refugiums (a separate, protected compartment in the filtration system) for nitrate and phosphate export, but it is also kept in display tanks for its ornamental appearance. Tangs and other herbivorous fish are known to graze it readily as a supplemental live food, making it popular in systems with a high herbivore population.

No copper-based medications should be introduced to any system containing macroalgae. Optional supplementation with magnesium, trace elements, and iron is generally not required in established reef systems but may support growth in nutrient-limited setups.

Other Gracilaria species kept in the hobby include Pom Pom (Gracilaria hayi), Thick Ogo (Gracilaria sp.), Green Ogo (Gracilaria tikvahiae), Monkey Finger (Gracilaria salicornia), Red Spaghetti (Gracilaria pacifica), and Flat-Branched Striped Gracilaria (Gracilaria mammaralis).

IMPORTANT — ATTACHMENT: As a red alga, Red Ogo will MELT at the base if buried in sand. Attach it to rock using superglue, zip-tie/fishing line, or wedging instead. Sand-bed placement is generally suitable only for green algae such as Caulerpa, Halimeda, Penicillus, Rhipocephalus, and Udotea, as well as some calcified Dasycladales.
breedingNotes: In captivity, propagation is achieved through simple fragmentation: portions of the thallus that detach or are clipped will form new growth points when placed in appropriate flow and lighting. Sexual reproduction via alternation of gametophyte and sporophyte generations occurs in the wild but is not a documented propagation path in hobby aquariums.

additional.name: Top Shelf Aquatics
additional.notes: Confirms refugium tumbling placement and nutrient-export function; limited numeric parameter data. Cross-verified placement and care approach.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros
additional.notes: Confirms scientific name Gracilaria parvispora and thin-branching growth form. Minimal husbandry data provided.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros general macroalgae care guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source — provides authoritative Mosaic-wide parameter targets (temp 22-26°C, pH 8.1-8.4, Ca 400-450 ppm, Mg 1250-1350 ppm, NO3 5-20 ppm, PO4 0.02-0.1 ppm) and the 4-method propagation standard. Added retroactively 2026-06-08.
